hypothesis: Incorporating SEM images from intermediate cycling stages (e.g., 25, 50, and 75 cycles) as an auxiliary pretraining domain between the additive-free source domain and the additive-containing target domain will create a morphological degradation continuum that reduces the representational gap during fine-tuning, thereby enabling the two-stage transfer learning framework to achieve equivalent classification performance (≥0.98 F1) on additive-containing aged cathodes with 50% fewer labeled target-domain samples than currently required.
